title: Feature: Tool Call Observability via WebSocket Event Log (ADR-2604012137)
description: Add lightweight tool-call event log to hex-nexus: SQLite tool_events table + POST /api/events endpoint + WebSocket broadcast to dashboard. Inspired by agents-observe pattern. Works without SpacetimeDB.

steps.description: Add tool_events table to SQLite schema in hex-nexus/src/adapters/sqlite_state.rs. Columns: id, session_id, agent_id, event_type, tool_name, input_json (truncated 4KB), result_json (truncated 4KB), exit_code, duration_ms, created_at. Add index on (session_id, created_at DESC).

Add POST /api/events route to hex-nexus. Accepts {session_id, agent_id?, event_type, tool_name?, input_json?, result_json?, exit_code?, duration_ms?}. Writes to tool_events SQLite table. Returns {id, created_at}.
Add GET /api/events?session_id=<id>&limit=100 route. Returns tool_events rows ordered by created_at DESC. Used for initial dashboard load (before WebSocket catches up).

Extend hex-nexus WebSocket broadcast to include tool_events. On each INSERT into tool_events, broadcast {type: 'tool_event', data: <row>} to all connected dashboard WebSocket clients subscribed to the relevant session_id.

Add hex hook observe <pre|post> subcommand to hex-cli/src/commands/hook.rs. Reads hook event JSON from stdin (same as existing hook commands), extracts tool_name + input/result, POSTs to /api/events non-blocking (fire and forget, 100ms timeout). Used as a standalone non-blocking hook.
Add TTL cleanup: delete tool_events older than 7 days. Run as part of hex-nexus startup cleanup routine (same location as stale agent cleanup). Prevents unbounded growth.

Add Activity panel to hex dashboard (hex-nexus/assets/src/). Solid.js component showing tool-call timeline: PreToolUse+PostToolUse pairs collapsed into one row, expandable input/output, session filter, agent filter, latency column. Subscribes to WebSocket tool_event messages and loads initial data from GET /api/events.

Update hex-cli/assets/templates/hex-claude-settings.json to add hex hook observe pre/post as non-blocking hooks alongside the enforcement hooks from ADR-2604012110. Document in hooks-prototype/README.md.
